program:
/*
# AppIsland — a whole Declare program running inside a box

`AppIsland` embeds a live Declare application: give it a `program` and it mounts
that program as its own child app — its own reactive graph, input router, and
stage — sized to this view's box. There is no coordinate sync: the tenant fills
the box, so your width/height constraints size it like any view.

It composes `DOMIsland` (the sanctioned foreign-content leaf) and builds the
host's mount key from structured inputs, so callers never hand-splice the
`run:…` wire. The page host (`browser/host-client.js`) sees the key on the box,
resolves the program on its boot ladder — prewarm → cache → in-browser compile,
identical on the dev server and a static host — and renders the child.

- `program` — what to run: a program name, or a relative path
  (`"../../calendar/calendar"` reaches the real app, no copy), or the reserved
  `"__raw__"` live-edit channel (the running program is the host app's
  `liveSource`, recompiled on change). `""` detaches the island — nothing mounts.
- `env` — the embedding environment, in query form (`"dark=1&base=../x/"`),
  delivered to the child's reactive `app.env` at mount and on every change.
  Empty sends none, so the child keeps its own defaults (e.g. system appearance).
- `childName` (inherited from `DOMIsland`) — the reverse channel: the running
  child's `appName` reflected back up, so a host can title itself by what it shows.

The window frame, activation, and titling belong to the caller — a desktop window
adds an `onMouseDown` that raises itself and reads `childName` for its title; the
homepage and the viewer just size the box. This primitive is only the embedding.
*/

class AppIsland extends DOMIsland [
    program: string = "",
    env: string = "",

    // the host mount key: `run:<program>`, with the env query after `|`. No
    // program → empty slot → the island stays detached (DOMIsland unmounts it).

    slot = { this.program == "" ? "" : "run:" + this.program + (this.env == "" ? "" : "|" + this.env) },
    ]

/*
# Declare docs

The Declare reference, rendered AS a Declare app — self-hosted: Declare
documenting Declare. It loads docs-model.json — the extractor's output
(tools/internal/doc/extract.mjs), one walkable data structure — and renders a
navigable class reference: a left rail of components, a detail pane with each
class's Markdown prose and an attributes table, every doc string rendered by
the runtime Markdown component. This one app is both the in-browser docs and
(once built) the web docs; the live object browser is this same view reached
from an instance.

`{ }` bodies are TypeScript → `0x…` colors and expressions; `[ ]` literal
slots take `#…` / named colors. Every color reads `theme.<token>` — one
prevailing record on the App (see it there), so the whole document reskins
from one place.
*/

font Sans [ family = "system-ui" ]

font Code [ family = "ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, monospace" ]   // matches the runtime house code face (PROSE.mono)

/*
## Type tokens

Selection is a PREVAILING slot: these tokens don't opt in themselves — the
detail pane sets `selectable = true` on its container, so all right-pane text
(including Markdown prose) is selectable, while the left rail stays
unselectable chrome.
*/

class Title extends Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
    fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text },
    ]


class Muted extends Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
    textColor = { this.theme.muted },
    ]


class Mono extends Text [ fontFamily = [Code, "ui-monospace", "monospace"] ]

// a component tab in the left rail — selection is one App attribute
// (`app.selected`, the class name); a tab lights when it matches and sets it
// on click, so the whole navigator is one string

class ClassTab extends View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 32, cornerRadius = 6,
    cname:   string = "",
    hovered: boolean = false,
    fill = { this.root.selected == classroot.cname ? this.theme.sel : this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
    onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
    onClick()     { this.root.refAt = classroot.cname; this.root.location = "reference/" + classroot.cname; this.root.showPage() },
    lbl: Mono [ x = 12, y = 8, fontSize = 14, wrap = false,
        text = { classroot.cname },
        textColor = { this.root.selected == classroot.cname ? this.theme.text : this.theme.tabIdle } ],
    ]


/*
## Reference rows

One row per documented member. `AttrRow` is an attribute: the signature line
(name : type = default), the prevailing / read-only badges, then its prose.
`MethodRow` and `EventRow` are the method and event forms — the full
signature with the name emphasized (a method), or the handler signature
(`onClick()`, an event), then the prose.
*/

class AttrRow extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    aname: string = { :name },              // the attribute's name — the jump index matches on it
    live:  boolean = false,                 // page-active, threaded down to any inline island
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 6 ],

    // name  : type  = default   — the signature, mono, one line

    sig: View [ width = { parent.width },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 8 ],
        Mono [ fontSize = { 15 * this.root.fontScale }, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = { :name } ],
        Mono [ fontSize = { 15 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.accent2 }, text = { ": " + :type } ],
        Mono [ fontSize = { 14 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.sigDim },
            text = { :default != null ? "= " + :default : "" } ],
        ],

    // prevailing / read-only badges, only when either holds

    flags: Mono [ fontSize = { 12 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.eyebrow },
        visible = { :prevailing || :readOnly },
        text = { [:prevailing ? "prevailing" : "", :readOnly ? "read-only" : ""].filter(Boolean).join("  ·  ") } ],

    // the captured doc — prose + any inline runnable islands; dimmed for the reference

    Segment [ datapath = :docSegs[], width = { parent.width },
        live = { classroot.live }, tone = { this.theme.refDim }, lead = 1.6 ],
    ]


// The signature string (`set(path: string, v: unknown): void`) comes whole from
// the model; a `:sig.slice()` would be MIS-READ as a datapath (the `.` extends
// the path), so the datapath values are captured into plain attributes first
// and sliced in JS.

class MethodRow extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    aname:    string = { :name },
    sigfull:  string = { :signature },
    tailtext: string = { this.sigfull.slice(this.aname.length) },   // "(path: …): void"
    live:     boolean = false,
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 6 ],

    sig: View [ width = { parent.width },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 0 ],
        Mono [ fontSize = { 15 * this.root.fontScale }, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = { classroot.aname } ],
        Mono [ fontSize = { 15 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.mtail }, text = { classroot.tailtext } ],
        ],

    Segment [ datapath = :docSegs[], width = { parent.width },
        live = { classroot.live }, tone = { this.theme.refDim }, lead = 1.6 ],
    ]


class EventRow extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    aname: string = { :name },
    live:  boolean = false,
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 6 ],

    sig: Mono [ fontSize = { 15 * this.root.fontScale }, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = { :signature } ],

    Segment [ datapath = :docSegs[], width = { parent.width },
        live = { classroot.live }, tone = { this.theme.refDim }, lead = 1.6 ],
    ]


/*
## The live example

An editable source field beside a running preview — the SAME machinery as the
homepage's editable demos: a `TextInput` seeded from the demo source that
republishes edits on `app.liveSource`, and an `AppIsland` the host mounts
the compiled child app into. Both are keyed by the CLASS NAME: the editor
seeds from `app.demoSources[<Class>]` (the server populates this from
apps/docs/demos/), and the island's `program = <Class>` is what the host
wires.
*/

class DocField extends TextInput [ multiline = true, spellcheck = false, wrap = false,
    fontFamily = [Code, "ui-monospace", "monospace"], fontSize = { 13 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.code },   // 13 = the house code size (PROSE.codeSize)
    editName: string = "",
    src: string = "",

    // Seed the field from the demo source once the subtree exists. (TextInput's
    // `initial` yielding-seed can't be used here: its attach-time guard fires
    // before this field's datapath-sourced `src` constraint is bound, so the seed
    // is skipped. An `onInit` write lands after the model has resolved `src`, and —
    // being an author write, not a constraint — leaves the field freely editable.)

    onInit() { this.text = this.src },
    onInput() { this.root.liveCard = this.editName; this.root.liveSource = this.text },
    ]


// Rendered by datapath replication over the class's 0-or-1 `example` array, so the
// whole island (editor + preview) is CONSTRUCTED only for classes that ship a demo.

class Example extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    cname:  string = { :name },
    elines: number = { :lines },
    esrc:   string = { :source },
    estage: number = { :stageH || 250 },   // extractor-measured stage height — the frame FITS the settled app
    live:   boolean = false,             // the parent gates the preview: it mounts only when this is true

    // this island's edit failed to compile (host publishes the report; see RunBlock)

    broken: boolean = { this.root.liveCard == this.cname && this.root.liveReport != "" },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 0 ],   // the preview frame abuts the code block below, no gap

    head: View [ width = { parent.width },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
        rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        gap:  View [ height = 8 ],
        Title [ fontSize = { 20 * this.root.fontScale }, text = "Example" ],
        ],

    gap12: View [ height = 12 ],         // the title's breathing room (the layout spacing is now 0)

    // the editable source card — coheres with a static code block (the same
    // `chipBg` tint, 8px radius, 13px code face) but is marked LIVE/EDITABLE by an
    // AMBER left bar + label, where a read-only block carries the teal `codeRule`.

    panel: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = { this.theme.chipBg },
        height = { Math.min(classroot.elines * 15 * this.root.fontScale + 105, 900) },   // 15px/line measured; field pad + 60/8 chrome
        bar:   View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = { classroot.broken ? this.theme.errBar : 0xE6B450 } ],
        fname: Mono [ x = 18, y = 10, fontSize = { 11 * this.root.fontScale }, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.strong },
            text = { classroot.cname + ".declare" } ],

        // the marker is an INVERTED chip — the amber accent as fill, black letters —
        // so "live/editable" reads at a glance, distinct from a read-only block

        tag:   View [ y = 7, height = 17, cornerRadius = 3, fill = 0xE6B450, x = { parent.width - this.width - 14 },
            lbl: Mono [ x = 7, y = 3, fontSize = 10, letterSpacing = 1, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = 0x1A1A1A,
                text = "LIVE · EDITABLE", wrap = false ],
            width = { this.lbl.width + 14 },
            ],

        // Revert — restore the pristine source and republish (see RunBlock)

        rev: View [ y = 5, height = 20, cornerRadius = 6, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            x = { parent.tag.x - this.width - 10 },
            width = { this.rlbl.width + 16 },
            fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
            onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
            onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
            onClick() { classroot.panel.field.text = classroot.esrc
                        this.root.liveCard = classroot.cname
                        this.root.liveSource = classroot.esrc },
            rlbl: Text [ x = 8, y = 3, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 11, fontWeight = semibold,
                textColor = { parent.hovered ? this.theme.text : this.theme.muted }, text = "Revert" ],
            ],

        // a line of breathing room below the label before the code starts

        hair:  View [ x = 18, y = 51, width = { parent.width - 30 }, height = 1, fill = { this.theme.line } ],
        field: DocField [ x = 18, y = 60, width = { parent.width - 30 }, height = { parent.height - 68 },
            editName = { classroot.cname }, src = { classroot.esrc } ],
        ],

    // the compile report at the island (see RunBlock — same teaching surface)

    err: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = { this.theme.errBg },
        visible = { classroot.broken },
        height = { this.msg.height + 20 },
        bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = { this.theme.errBar } ],
        msg: Text [ x = 18, y = 10, width = { parent.width - 36 }, wrap = true,
            fontFamily = [Code, "ui-monospace", "monospace"], fontSize = { 12 * this.root.fontScale },
            textColor = { this.theme.errText }, text = { this.root.liveReport } ],
        ],

    // the live preview island — the host mounts the compiled child app here. The slot
    // is live ONLY while the parent says so (`live`), so exactly one preview (the one
    // on the visible page) compiles at a time, even though every page is constructed.

    stage: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.estage }, fill = { this.theme.chipBg },
        run:     AppIsland [ width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
            program = { classroot.live ? classroot.cname : "" } ],

        // a blue rule (a darker shade of the chipBg box tint) divides the code above from the
        // running app below; the gold spine (drawn OVER both) continues the code's gold left bar
        // down the preview, same x/width

        divider: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        spine:   View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = 0xE6B450 ],
        ],
    ]


/*
## Inline examples, woven through the prose

Every `declare` code fence in any prose that compiles (as-is, or wrapped in
`App […]`) becomes a `RunBlock`: the source above, its live preview below —
edit and it re-runs. The extractor wrote its source to
`apps/docs/demos/<id>.declare`, so the host seeds and mounts it exactly
like the homepage's demos. `live` (threaded from the page) keeps the
preview's slot empty until its page is the one on screen, so only the visible
page's islands ever compile.
*/

class RunBlock extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    rid:    string  = { :id },
    rsrc:   string  = { :source },
    rlines: number  = { :lines },
    rstage: number  = { :stageH || 200 },   // extractor-measured stage height — the frame FITS the settled app
    live:   boolean = false,

    // this island's edit is the one that failed to compile — the host publishes the
    // report on app.liveReport (cleared by a clean compile), keyed by app.liveCard

    broken: boolean = { this.root.liveCard == this.rid && this.root.liveReport != "" },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 0 ],   // the preview frame abuts the code block, no gap

    // same code-box grammar as a static block (chipBg tint, 8px radius, 13px face),
    // marked live/editable by the amber bar + label (a read-only block is teal).

    // height: measured 15px/line at the 13px code face, plus the field's ~20px
    // padding and the 46+8 header/footer chrome — so the code fits without an
    // inner scrollbar up to the cap

    editor: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = { this.theme.chipBg },
        height = { Math.min(classroot.rlines * 15 * this.root.fontScale + 93, 900) },
        bar:  View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = { classroot.broken ? this.theme.errBar : 0xE6B450 } ],
        tag:  View [ x = 18, y = 7, height = 17, cornerRadius = 3, fill = 0xE6B450,
            lbl: Mono [ x = 7, y = 3, fontSize = 10, letterSpacing = 1, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = 0x1A1A1A,
                text = "LIVE · EDITABLE", wrap = false ],
            width = { this.lbl.width + 14 },
            ],

        // Revert restores the pristine source and republishes it — the way back the
        // guide owes a reader it just told to break something

        rev: View [ y = 5, height = 20, cornerRadius = 6, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            x = { parent.width - this.width - 10 },
            width = { this.rlbl.width + 16 },
            fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
            onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
            onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
            onClick() { classroot.editor.field.text = classroot.rsrc
                        this.root.liveCard = classroot.rid
                        this.root.liveSource = classroot.rsrc },
            rlbl: Text [ x = 8, y = 3, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 11, fontWeight = semibold,
                textColor = { parent.hovered ? this.theme.text : this.theme.muted }, text = "Revert" ],
            ],
        hint: Muted [ y = 9, fontSize = { 11 * this.root.fontScale }, italic = true, text = "runs live below ↓",
            x = { parent.rev.x - this.width - 12 } ],

        // a line of breathing room below the label before the code starts

        field: DocField [ x = 18, y = 46, width = { parent.width - 30 }, height = { parent.height - 54 },
            editName = { classroot.rid }, src = { classroot.rsrc } ],
        ],

    // the compile report, at the island — the diagnostic is a teaching surface, so a
    // broken edit shows its error right here (the last good render stays below it)

    err: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = { this.theme.errBg },
        visible = { classroot.broken },
        height = { this.msg.height + 20 },
        bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = { this.theme.errBar } ],
        msg: Text [ x = 18, y = 10, width = { parent.width - 36 }, wrap = true,
            fontFamily = [Code, "ui-monospace", "monospace"], fontSize = { 12 * this.root.fontScale },
            textColor = { this.theme.errText }, text = { this.root.liveReport } ],
        ],

    stage: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.rstage }, fill = { this.theme.chipBg },
        run:     AppIsland [ width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
            program = { classroot.live ? classroot.rid : "" } ],
        divider: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        spine:   View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = 0xE6B450 ],
        ],
    ]


// one prose segment — a run of Markdown text OR (0-or-1) a runnable island. A code
// block that didn't compile stays folded into the Markdown as a static code fence.
// `tone`/`lead` let a caller dim the prose (the reference runs dimmer than the guide).

class Segment extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    live: boolean = false,
    tone: Color   = { this.theme.body },
    lead: number  = 1.6,
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
    md: Markdown [ width = { parent.width }, lineHeight = { classroot.lead }, bodyColor = { classroot.tone }, scale = { this.root.fontScale }, dark = { this.root.darkUI },
        visible = { :md != "" }, text = { :md },
        onLink(href) { this.root.openDocLink(href) },
        ],
    RunBlock [ datapath = :code[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.live } ],
    ]


/*
## The class page

One class's whole detail page — shown only when it is the selection. All the
detail pages overlay at (0,0); `visible` picks the selected one, so switching
is a single boolean and the scroll pane measures only the live page.
*/

class ClassDetail extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    cname: string = "",
    visible = { this.root.selected == classroot.cname },

    // this page is on screen — the gate every inline preview reads before it mounts

    pageLive: boolean = { this.root.mode == "reference" && this.root.selected == classroot.cname },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 18 ],

    // Jump: find the member row (attribute, method, or event) carrying this name and
    // scroll it into view. Imperative, so it lives in the click handler (View.reveal,
    // the runtime scroll-into-view primitive); every row carries an `aname` to match.

    revealAttr(name) {
        for (const box of [this.attrs, this.meths, this.evts]) {
            for (const row of box.children) {
                if (row.aname === name) { row.scrollIntoView(); return }
            }
        }
        },

    // header: the class name + what it extends

    head: View [ width = { parent.width },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 6 ],
        Title [ fontSize = { 32 * this.root.fontScale }, text = { classroot.cname } ],
        Mono  [ fontSize = { 14 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.sigDim },
            text = { :extends != null ? "class " + classroot.cname + " extends " + :extends
                                      : "class " + classroot.cname + "  (base)" } ],
        ],

    // the member jump index — one wrap of chips (attributes teal, methods blue, events
    // amber), click to scroll to a definition below. All three kinds share `revealAttr`.

    index: View [ width = { parent.width },
        visible = { :attributes.length > 0 || :methods.length > 0 || :events.length > 0 },
        layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 8, lineSpacing = 8 ],

        // attribute chips

        View [ datapath = :attributes[],
            aname: string = { :name },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            height = 26, cornerRadius = 6, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.chipHov : this.theme.chipBg },
            onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
            onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
            onClick() { classroot.revealAttr(this.aname) },
            lbl: Mono [ x = 10, y = 5, fontSize = { 13 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.chipTeal }, text = { parent.aname } ],
            width = { this.lbl.width + 20 },
            ],

        // method chips — name()

        View [ datapath = :methods[],
            aname: string = { :name },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            height = 26, cornerRadius = 6, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.mchipHov : this.theme.mchipBg },
            onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
            onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
            onClick() { classroot.revealAttr(this.aname) },
            lbl: Mono [ x = 10, y = 5, fontSize = { 13 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.chipBlue }, text = { parent.aname + "()" } ],
            width = { this.lbl.width + 20 },
            ],

        // event chips — the handler signature

        View [ datapath = :events[],
            aname: string = { :name },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            height = 26, cornerRadius = 6, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.echipHov : this.theme.echipBg },
            onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
            onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
            onClick() { classroot.revealAttr(this.aname) },
            lbl: Mono [ x = 10, y = 5, fontSize = { 13 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.chipAmber }, text = { :signature } ],
            width = { this.lbl.width + 20 },
            ],
        ],

    // the class prose — dimmed, with any inline runnable examples woven in

    Segment [ datapath = :docSegs[], width = { parent.width },
        live = { classroot.pageLive }, tone = { this.theme.refDim }, lead = 1.6 ],

    // attributes (hidden for a class that documents none — e.g. Dataset)

    attrsHead: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :attributes.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
        rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        gap:  View [ height = 8 ],
        Title [ fontSize = { 20 * this.root.fontScale }, text = "Attributes" ],
        ],
    attrs: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :attributes.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
        AttrRow [ datapath = :attributes[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.pageLive } ],
        ],

    // methods (hidden for a class that documents none)

    methsHead: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :methods.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
        rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        gap:  View [ height = 8 ],
        Title [ fontSize = { 20 * this.root.fontScale }, text = "Methods" ],
        ],
    meths: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :methods.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
        MethodRow [ datapath = :methods[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.pageLive } ],
        ],

    // events (hidden for a class that documents none)

    evtsHead: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :events.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
        rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.rule } ],
        gap:  View [ height = 8 ],
        Title [ fontSize = { 20 * this.root.fontScale }, text = "Events" ],
        ],
    evts: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :events.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
        EventRow [ datapath = :events[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.pageLive } ],
        ],

    // the live, editable example — replicated 0-or-1 times, so it's built only when
    // the class ships a demo (apps/docs/demos/<Class>.declare). Its preview is
    // live only while this class is the reference selection.

    exampleBox: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :example.length > 0 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 18 ],
        Example [ datapath = :example[], width = { parent.width },
            live = { this.root.mode == "reference" && this.root.selected == classroot.cname } ],
        ],

    tail: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 48 ],
    ]


/*
## The guide

The mode switch, the chapter rail, and a chapter page. The doc app is two
views over one model: a narrative GUIDE and the class REFERENCE. `app.mode`
picks which; each keeps its own selection (`app.chapter` / `app.selected`) so
switching back lands where you left.
*/

// The mode switch is a full-width segmented pair — each tab half the rail, label
// centered — so the rail's primary control reads at the rail's own (fixed) type
// size. Nav chrome does NOT ride app.fontScale: the A-stepper is a reading-comfort
// control for the content pane; the rail keeps its designed sizes.

class ModeTab extends View [ height = 30, cornerRadius = 8,
    label: string = "",
    m: string = "",
    hovered: boolean = false,
    fill = { this.root.mode == classroot.m ? this.theme.modeOn : this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
    onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
    onClick()     { this.root.location = classroot.m == "guide" ? "guide/" + this.root.guideAt : "reference/" + this.root.refAt; this.root.showPage() },
    lbl: Text [ y = 7, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, wrap = false,
        x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 },
        text = { classroot.label },
        textColor = { this.root.mode == classroot.m ? this.theme.text : this.theme.modeOff } ],
    ]


// one chapter link in the guide rail — lights when it is the current chapter

class ChapterTab extends View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 32, cornerRadius = 6,
    cid: string = { :id },
    cnum: number = { :num },
    ctitle: string = { :short },
    hovered: boolean = false,
    fill = { this.root.chapter == classroot.cid ? this.theme.sel : this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
    onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
    onClick()     { this.root.guideAt = classroot.cid; this.root.location = "guide/" + classroot.cid; this.root.showPage() },

    // numbered, because the chapters cite each other by number ("chapter 3")

    lbl: Text [ x = 12, y = 8, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 14, wrap = false,
        text = { classroot.cnum + ". " + classroot.ctitle },
        textColor = { this.root.chapter == classroot.cid ? this.theme.text : this.theme.tabIdle } ],
    ]


// one Part in the guide rail — an eyebrow over that part's chapter links

class PartGroup extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 3 ],
    p: string = { :part },
    g:       View [ height = 14 ],
    eyebrow: Mono [ fontSize = 12, letterSpacing = 1.4, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, text = { classroot.p.toUpperCase() } ],
    g2:      View [ height = 4 ],
    ChapterTab [ datapath = :chapters[] ],
    ]


// one chapter page — the chapter as ordered segments (prose + inline runnable islands),
// then its optional flagship demo. Every ```declare block that compiles is a live island.

class ChapterDetail extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    cid: string = { :id },
    visible = { this.root.mode == "guide" && this.root.chapter == classroot.cid },
    pageLive: boolean = { this.root.mode == "guide" && this.root.chapter == classroot.cid },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 20 ],

    // The chapter's content is its OWN file (chapters/<id>.json — the
    // extractor's per-chapter projection), fetched EAGERLY at construction:
    // the model carries only the spine, first paint waits only for the
    // chapter the location names, and the rest stream in behind it — so
    // switches are instant once landed, and a future in-app search can
    // derive over the whole resident fleet. The crawl pulls these files
    // through its data resolver; a missing one fails the build loudly.

    src: DataSource [ ],
    onInit() { this.src.url = "chapters/" + :id + ".json"; this.src.fetch() },

    loading: Text [ visible = { classroot.src.loading }, fontSize = { 14 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, text = "Loading the chapter…" ],
    failed:  Text [ visible = { classroot.src.failed }, fontSize = { 14 * this.root.fontScale }, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
        text = { "The chapter did not load — " + (classroot.src.error || "") } ],

    // the guide is the primary reading surface: brighter body, looser leading (Segment defaults)

    content: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { classroot.src.loaded }, datapath = { classroot.src.value },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 20 ],

        Segment [ datapath = :segs[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.pageLive } ],

        // a flagship runnable example the chapter opts into with `<!-- demo: <Class> -->`

        demoBox: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { :demo.length > 0 },
            Example [ datapath = :demo[], width = { parent.width }, live = { classroot.pageLive } ],
            ],
        ],
    tail: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 60 ],
    ]


/*
## The font-size stepper

Three differently-sized "A"s — small · reset · large. Each cell is a centered
"A" whose glyph size signals the action: the small A shrinks, the middle A
resets to the default, the large A grows. The glyphs are FIXED (not
`* app.fontScale`) so the control itself never zooms. Every doc `fontSize`
reads `{ N * app.fontScale }` (and Markdown takes `scale`), so one attribute
zooms the whole document.
*/

class SizeBtn extends View [ width = 32, height = 30, cornerRadius = 6,
    glyph: number = 14,
    delta: number = 0,          // delta 0 = reset to the default
    hovered: boolean = false,
    fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
    onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
    onClick() { this.root.fontScale = classroot.delta == 0 ? 1.0
                    : Math.max(0.7, Math.min(1.6, Math.round((this.root.fontScale + classroot.delta) * 100) / 100)) },
    lbl: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontWeight = semibold,
        fontSize = { classroot.glyph }, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, text = "A",
        x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 }, y = { (parent.height - this.height) / 2 } ],
    ]


// the theme switch — ☀ light · ◐ follow the system · ☾ dark. Selection is one
// App attribute (`themeMode`); the theme record derives from it, so the whole
// app (and every island's chrome) reskins from this row.

class ThemeBtn extends View [ width = 32, height = 30, cornerRadius = 6,
    m:     string = "",
    glyph: string = "",
    hovered: boolean = false,
    fill = { this.root.themeMode == this.m ? this.theme.modeOn : this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null },
    onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
    onClick() { this.root.themeMode = classroot.m },
    lbl: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 14,
        text = { classroot.glyph },
        textColor = { this.root.themeMode == classroot.m ? this.theme.text : this.theme.muted },
        x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 }, y = { (parent.height - this.height) / 2 } ],
    ]


/*
## The app

A fixed left rail plus a scrolling detail pane, over the model.
*/

App [ fill = { this.theme.bg }, appName = "Declare Docs",

    /* Navigation is ONE string — `location`, the URL fragment (docs/system-design/location.md).
       The grammar is this app's own: `<mode>/<cursor>[@heading]` (`guide/04-tree`,
       `reference/Slider`, `guide/04-tree@named-views`). The declared initial is the
       default, so a fresh `docs.declare` shows the first guide chapter with a clean
       URL (§3). mode/chapter/selected DERIVE from it and are NEVER assigned — every
       tab and link writes `location`, back/forward writes it back, state follows
       (the single-writer rule; assigning derived state would disconnect history). */

    location = "guide/01-thinking-in-declare",

    /* Cross-mode memory (NOT a language feature — two plain remembered cursors).
       `location` only carries the ACTIVE mode's cursor, so switching guide↔reference
       would forget the other side. The tab handlers keep these in step and the mode
       switch consults them, so returning to a mode lands where you left it. */

    guideAt: string = "01-thinking-in-declare",              // last guide chapter visited …
    refAt:   string = "View",                  // … and last reference class

    // The active view, parsed out of `location` (the anchor stripped first — it
    // selects a viewpoint, not a page). The inactive mode's cursor falls back to its
    // remembered value, so its rail/selection stay put behind the other mode.

    mode:     string = { this.root.location.split("@")[0].split("/")[0] || "guide" },
    chapter:  string = { this.root.mode == "guide" ? (this.root.location.split("@")[0].split("/")[1] || "01-thinking-in-declare") : this.root.guideAt },
    selected: string = { this.root.mode == "reference" ? (this.root.location.split("@")[0].split("/")[1] || "View") : this.root.refAt },

    fontScale: number = 1.0,                   // reader font-size zoom for the CONTENT pane (the A stepper drives it; nav is fixed)
    themeMode: string = "system",              // "light" | "system" | "dark" — the ☀◐☾ switch; system follows app.dark

    // the ONE resolved fact every color decision reads — the theme record AND
    // the Markdown rich-element palette must agree, or an override leaves
    // headings in the OS scheme while the chrome switches (seen in the wild)

    darkUI: boolean = { this.themeMode == "dark" || (this.themeMode == "system" && this.root.dark) },

    // the rail is fixed-width chrome: its type no longer rides the reading zoom,
    // and the chapter slugs are short by design, so 232 sits snug around them

    railW: number = 236,
    liveCard: string = "",                     // the demo an inline editor is republishing (name) …
    liveSource: string = "",                   // … and its current source — the host recompiles this pair live

    // (app.liveReport — BUILT-IN — carries the host's rendered compile report; islands display it)

    /* The whole palette in one prevailing record, switched by the OS color
       scheme (`app.dark`, fed live by the runtime) — every color below reads
       `theme.<token>`, so the docs render dark or light and follow the system
       theme as it flips. The dark set is the original tuned palette; the
       live-preview canvas stays a fixed dark in both themes (the demos are
       dark-authored examples). */

    theme = { this.darkUI
        ? ({ bg: 0x0B141B, rail: 0x0D1922, surface: 0x0C1620, chipBg: 0x14232E, hover: 0x152633, sel: 0x1C2D39, mchipBg: 0x172436, echipBg: 0x241F12, modeOn: 0x1F3B54, chipHov: 0x1C3243, mchipHov: 0x213649, echipHov: 0x332B18, line: 0x1E313C, rule: 0x345B84, edge: 0x1B2C39, accent: 0x4C8DFF, accent2: 0x37E0C8, chipTeal: 0x9FD9CE, chipBlue: 0xA9C7FF, chipAmber: 0xE7C98A, text: 0xE7EEF2, body: 0xC4CFD8, code: 0xC7D3DC, strong: 0xB7C6D2, mtail: 0x9FB3C2, tabIdle: 0x9BB0BE, refDim: 0x98A7B1, muted: 0x8A9BA6, modeOff: 0x7C93A2, sigDim: 0x6E8494, eyebrow: 0x55707F, tag: 0x4F6472, errBg: 0x2A1518, errBar: 0xE0525E, errText: 0xF2A0A6 })
        : ({ bg: 0xF6F8FA, rail: 0xECF0F4, surface: 0xFFFFFF, chipBg: 0xE9EFF4, hover: 0xE4EBF1, sel: 0xDBE5EE, mchipBg: 0xE7EDF6, echipBg: 0xF5EFE0, modeOn: 0xD1DFF0, chipHov: 0xD9E4ED, mchipHov: 0xD4DEEF, echipHov: 0xECE3CD, line: 0xDCE3E9, rule: 0xB6C8DA, edge: 0xE0E6EC, accent: 0x2F6FE0, accent2: 0x12A08D, chipTeal: 0x15806E, chipBlue: 0x3355C2, chipAmber: 0x8F6F28, text: 0x172530, body: 0x2E3B46, code: 0x2B3843, strong: 0x3A4A56, mtail: 0x4F5F6B, tabIdle: 0x55636F, refDim: 0x51606C, muted: 0x6A7883, modeOff: 0x74828D, sigDim: 0x84929D, eyebrow: 0x93A0AA, tag: 0xA2AEB8, errBg: 0xFBE9EA, errBar: 0xC93B47, errText: 0x8E2830 }) },

    /* Code-vs-prose coherence, matching the source-viewer reader: prose (and
       every non-code block) wraps to a reading measure while code fills the
       column — so code renders WIDER than prose — and every code block gets a
       tinted box with a teal left bar. All prevailing, so every
       Markdown/HTMLText below inherits it. */

    richTextLayout = { { default: { maxWidth: 560 * this.root.fontScale }, code: { maxWidth: 0 } } },
    codeBackground = { this.theme.chipBg },
    codeRule = { this.theme.accent2 },

    docs: DataSource [ url = "docs-model.json" ],
    datapath = { this.docs.value },                 // root every :path below into the model
    onInit() { this.docs.fetch() },            // DataSource is explicit-fetch — kick it once, on construction

    /* The `declare-docs:` resolver — this app's packaging of the one symbolic
       link scheme (tools/doc/links.mjs is the registry; the gate keeps every
       target real). Guide and reference IDs navigate IN-APP; the rest resolve
       by the same mechanical id→path rule the registry generates them with, and
       leave through `navigate`. An ordinary href just navigates. */

    // A page switch starts at the TOP of the new page — the detail pane keeps its
    // scroll offset otherwise. The reveal target is a zero-size anchor at the pane's
    // very top, NOT the column: the column sits 40px into the pane, so aligning ITS
    // top with the viewport would eat that margin. Every switch path routes through
    // here: rail tabs and prose links alike.

    showPage() { this.detail.top.scrollIntoView() },

    // An in-app doc link is a location WRITE (docs/system-design/location.md §5) — it navigates
    // the app exactly like a rail tab, so it deep-links, shares, and back-buttons. It
    // also keeps the remembered cursor in step (like the tabs). External schemes still
    // leave through `navigate`.

    openDocLink(href) {
        if (!href.startsWith("declare-docs:")) { this.root.navigate(href); return }
        const id = href.slice("declare-docs:".length)
        if (id.startsWith("guide:")) {                       // guide:tree → the chapter whose id ends with the slug
            const slug = id.slice("guide:".length)
            const hit = (this.root.docs.read(["guide"]) || []).find(c => c.id.replace(/^\d+-/, "") == slug)
            if (hit) { this.root.guideAt = hit.id; this.root.location = "guide/" + hit.id; this.root.showPage() }
            return
        }
        if (id == "reference:index") { this.root.location = "reference/" + this.root.refAt; this.root.showPage(); return }
        if (!id.includes(":")) {                             // a reference symbol: View / View.width
            this.root.refAt = id.split(".")[0]; this.root.location = "reference/" + this.root.refAt; this.root.showPage(); return
        }
        if (id.startsWith("operational:")) { this.root.navigate("docs/operational/" + id.slice("operational:".length) + ".md"); return }
        if (id == "spec:core") { this.root.navigate("docs/declare.md"); return }
        if (id == "essay:why-declare") { this.root.navigate("apps/homepage/homepage.declare") }
        },

    /* ## The left rail
       Brand, the Guide/Reference switch, the font-size stepper, then the nav
       for the live mode. */

    rail: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { this.root.railW }, height = { this.root.hostHeight }, scrolls = true, fill = { this.theme.rail },
        edge: View [ x = { this.root.railW - 1 }, y = 0, width = 1, height = { this.root.hostHeight }, fill = { this.theme.edge } ],
        col: View [ x = 16, y = 22, width = { this.root.railW - 32 },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 4 ],

            // the brand, one line — "Declare" in the gradient, "Docs" solid: the same
            // rendition Declare Calendar uses for its own name

            brand: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 23,

                // the house brand rendition, x-aligned with Calendar's and Viewer's
                // (their bars start the brand at 20; this rail's column starts at 16)

                row: View [ x = 4, height = 22,
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 5 ],

                    // "Declare" is the way home — navigate() resolves against the
                    // distro root, so this works on either host at any mount point

                    Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 18,
                        fontWeight = semibold, letterSpacing = { -0.02 * this.fontSize }, wrap = false,
                        textFill = { gradient("90deg", this.theme.accent, this.theme.accent2) }, text = "Declare",
                        onClick() { this.root.navigate("apps/homepage/homepage.declare") },
                        ],
                    Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 18,
                        fontWeight = semibold, letterSpacing = { -0.02 * this.fontSize }, wrap = false,
                        textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = "Docs" ],
                    ],
                ],
            g0:    View [ height = 12 ],
            modes: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 6 ],
                ModeTab [ label = "Guide", m = "guide", width = { (parent.width - 6) / 2 } ],
                ModeTab [ label = "Reference", m = "reference", width = { (parent.width - 6) / 2 } ],
                ],

            // font-size stepper — small / reset / large "A" glyphs zoom every text size

            g_sz:  View [ height = 8 ],
            ctl: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 8 ],
                size: View [ height = 30, cornerRadius = 8, fill = { this.theme.surface }, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 0 ],
                    SizeBtn [ glyph = 11, delta = -0.1 ],
                    div1: View [ width = 1, height = 18, y = 6, fill = { this.theme.line } ],
                    SizeBtn [ glyph = 15, delta = 0 ],
                    div2: View [ width = 1, height = 18, y = 6, fill = { this.theme.line } ],
                    SizeBtn [ glyph = 19, delta = 0.1 ],
                    width = { this.contentWidth },
                    ],
                mode: View [ height = 30, cornerRadius = 8, fill = { this.theme.surface }, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 0 ],
                    ThemeBtn [ glyph = "☀︎", m = "light" ],
                    div1: View [ width = 1, height = 18, y = 6, fill = { this.theme.line } ],
                    ThemeBtn [ glyph = "◐", m = "system" ],
                    div2: View [ width = 1, height = 18, y = 6, fill = { this.theme.line } ],
                    ThemeBtn [ glyph = "☾", m = "dark" ],
                    width = { this.contentWidth },
                    ],
                ],

            // guide nav — parts and their chapters

            guideNav: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { this.root.mode == "guide" },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
                PartGroup [ datapath = :guideParts[] ],
                ],

            // reference nav — the component list

            refNav: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { this.root.mode == "reference" },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 4 ],
                g1:    View [ height = 14 ],
                eyebrow: Mono [ fontSize = 12, letterSpacing = 1.4, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, text = "COMPONENTS" ],
                g2:    View [ height = 4 ],
                ClassTab [ datapath = :tree[], cname = :name ],
                ],
            ],
        ],

    /* ## The detail pane
       Its own scroller; guide chapters and class pages overlay, the mode +
       selection pick the one live page (so the scroller measures only it).
       `selectable` (prevailing) opts the whole right pane into ordinary
       browser text selection — prose, signatures, Markdown runs — while the
       rail stays chrome. */

    detail: View [ x = { this.root.railW }, y = 0, width = { this.root.hostWidth - this.root.railW }, height = { this.root.hostHeight }, scrolls = true, selectable = true,
        top: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1 ],       // showPage's scroll-to-top anchor
        col: View [ x = 48, y = 40, width = { parent.width - 96 },
            guideCol: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { this.root.mode == "guide" },
                ChapterDetail [ datapath = :guide[], cid = :id ],
                ],
            refCol: View [ width = { parent.width }, visible = { this.root.mode == "reference" },
                ClassDetail [ datapath = :tree[], cname = :name ],
                ],
            ],
        ],
    ]
